rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they refer to visible ports, and use implementation scopes only for output assignments or subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model in AGREE contracts; avoid package-qualified names not present in the model.
generic_rules: Write AGREE contracts with valid scalar types (int, real, bool) and avoid invalid references to unresolved types or features.
Ensure that guarantees relate visible inputs to outputs, and avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in type-level contracts.
Generate AGREE constants and equations using named visible features and legal AADL data types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and placed in appropriate AADL component type or implementation scopes.
Use `const`, `eq`, `ass`, and `gua` keywords with valid identifiers and types in AGREE syntax.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes; instead, move them to implementation scopes or delete invalid clauses.
Avoid referencing event ports as numeric or Boolean values unless explicitly defined as data ports in the AADL model.
